contentItems.title: Mechanism of Action: SGLT2 Inhibitors
contentItems.body: Detailed review of renal glucose transport pathways.
contentItems.summary: Understanding renal glucose reabsorption pathways and cardiovascular benefits in diabetic patients.

contentItems.title: Adverse Effects of Beta-Blockers
contentItems.body: Reviewing bronchoconstriction, bradycardia, and hypoglycemia mask.
contentItems.summary: High-yield review of autonomic pharmacology side effects.

contentItems.title: Neoplastic Transformations in Breast Tissue
contentItems.body: Examines genetic alterations, HER2 overexpression, and histological features.
contentItems.summary: Histopathology overview of ductal and lobular carcinomas.

contentItems.title: Pathological Presentation of Acute Glomerulonephritis
contentItems.body: Review of subepithelial humps, post-streptococcal renal lesions, and microscopy.
contentItems.summary: Renal pathology masterclass for PG aspirants.

contentItems.title: Anatomy of the Brachial Plexus
contentItems.body: Detailed review of roots, trunks, divisions, cords, and terminal branches.
contentItems.summary: Brachial plexus mapping and clinical nerve injury syndromes.

contentItems.title: Circle of Willis: Vascular Dissection
contentItems.body: Demonstrates anterior and posterior cerebral circulation pathways.
contentItems.summary: 3D dissection video mapping the vascular base of the brain.

contentItems.title: Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy Techniques
contentItems.body: Demonstrates hepatobiliary triangle identification and clip placement.
contentItems.summary: Step-by-step surgical techniques for gallbladder excision.

contentItems.title: Principles of Wound Healing and Closures
contentItems.body: First vs second intention, collagen synthesis timelines, and suture select guides.
contentItems.summary: Clinical review of surgical wound repair pathways.

contentItems.title: Ingested Article: Mechanism of SGLT2 Inhibitors
contentItems.body: This is a high-yield breakdown of SGLT2 and proximal tubule glucosuria mechanics.
contentItems.summary: Updated clinical guidelines for low expression profiles.

mcqs.question: Which of the following describes the mechanism of action of SGLT2 inhibitors in managing type 2 diabetes?

options.text: Stimulation of insulin release from pancreatic beta cells
Inhibition of glucose absorption in the small intestine
Inhibition of sodium-glucose cotransporter 2 in the renal proximal tubules, leading to glucosuria
Decreasing hepatic glucose production through AMPK activation

mcqs.explanation: SGLT2 inhibitors (e.g., empagliflozin, dapagliflozin) block the sodium-glucose cotransporter 2 in the renal proximal convoluted tubule, which prevents glucose reabsorption and promotes urinary excretion.
mcqs.subject: Pharmacology

mcqs.question: A patient on beta-blockers presents with hypoglycemia. Which symptom of hypoglycemia would be masked by this medication?
mcqs.explanation: Beta-blockers block adrenergic receptors and thus mask the sympathetic warning signs of hypoglycemia, most notably tachycardia and palpitations. Sweating is mediated by cholinergic pathway and is not masked.
mcqs.subject: Pharmacology

mcqs.question: Histopathology of a breast biopsy shows neoplastic cells filling the ducts without invading the basement membrane. What is the diagnosis?
mcqs.explanation: Ductal Carcinoma In Situ (DCIS) is characterized by neoplastic cells filling the lumens of ducts without invasion beyond the basement membrane.
mcqs.subject: Pathology

mcqs.question: Which histological finding is characteristic of Post-Streptococcal Acute Glomerulonephritis (PSGN) under electron microscopy?
mcqs.explanation: Electron microscopy in PSGN classicially reveals subepithelial immune complex deposits, described as 'humps' on the epithelial side of the basement membrane.
mcqs.subject: Pathology

mcqs.question: A patient presents with 'claw hand' deformity after an upper extremity trauma. Which part of the brachial plexus is most likely injured?
mcqs.explanation: Injury to the lower trunk (C8-T1) of the brachial plexus (e.g., Klumpke's palsy) affects the ulnar nerve fibers, leading to a claw hand deformity.
mcqs.subject: Anatomy

mcqs.question: Which artery connects the anterior cerebral circulation to the posterior cerebral circulation in the Circle of Willis?
mcqs.explanation: The posterior communicating artery (PCoA) connects the internal carotid system (anterior) with the posterior cerebral artery (posterior circulation).
mcqs.subject: Anatomy

mcqs.question: During a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, which boundaries define the Calot's (hepatobiliary) triangle?

options.text: Cystic duct, common hepatic duct, and inferior border of the liver
Common bile duct, cystic artery, and portal vein
Cystic duct, common bile duct, and duodenum
Right hepatic artery, left hepatic artery, and gallbladder bed

mcqs.explanation: Calot's triangle boundaries are: the cystic duct inferiorly, the common hepatic duct medially, and the inferior edge of the liver (or cystic artery) superiorly.
mcqs.subject: General Surgery

mcqs.question: Which phase of wound healing is characterized by collagen deposition and peak tensile strength acquisition?
mcqs.explanation: During the maturation/remodeling phase (starting from 3 weeks and continuing for months), type III collagen is replaced by type I collagen, and wound tensile strength peaks.
mcqs.subject: General Surgery
